# services.glusterfs

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.glusterfs`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.glusterfs.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable GlusterFS Daemon. |
| `services.glusterfs.enableGlustereventsd` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the GlusterFS Events Daemon |
| `services.glusterfs.extraFlags` | `list of string` | Extra flags passed to the GlusterFS daemon |
| `services.glusterfs.killMode` | `one of "control-group", "process", "mixed", "none"` | The systemd KillMode to use for glusterd. glusterd spawns other daemons like gsyncd. If you want these to stop when glusterd is stopped (e.g. to ensure that NixOS config changes are reflected even for these sub-daemons), set this to 'control-group'. If however you want running volume processes (glusterfsd) and thus gluster mounts not be interrupted when glusterd is restarted (for example, when you want to restart them manually at a later time), set this to 'process'. |
| `services.glusterfs.logLevel` | `one of "DEBUG", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR", "CRITICAL", "TRACE", "NONE"` | Log level used by the GlusterFS daemon |
| `services.glusterfs.stopKillTimeout` | `string` | The systemd TimeoutStopSec to use. After this time after having been asked to shut down, glusterd (and depending on the killMode setting also its child processes) are killed by systemd. The default is set low because GlusterFS (as of 3.10) is known to not tell its children (like gsyncd) to terminate at all. |
| `services.glusterfs.tlsSettings` | `null or (submodule)` | Make the server communicate via TLS. This means it will only connect to other gluster servers having certificates signed by the same CA. Enabling this will create a file {file}`/var/lib/glusterd/secure-access`. Disabling will delete this file again. See also: <https://gluster.readthedocs.io/en/latest/Administrator%20Guide/SSL/> |
| `services.glusterfs.tlsSettings.caCert` | `absolute path` | Path certificate authority used to sign the cluster certificates. |
| `services.glusterfs.tlsSettings.tlsKeyPath` | `string` | Path to the private key used for TLS. |
| `services.glusterfs.tlsSettings.tlsPem` | `absolute path` | Path to the certificate used for TLS. |
| `services.glusterfs.useRpcbind` | `boolean` | Enable use of rpcbind. This is required for Gluster's NFS functionality. You may want to turn it off to reduce the attack surface for DDoS reflection attacks. See <https://davelozier.com/glusterfs-and-rpcbind-portmap-ddos-reflection-attacks/> and <https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1426842> for details. |
